title: Setting up the Bookshelf
summary: Sara and her brother Tom are trying to build a flat-pack bookshelf in their new flat in Lisbon, but the instructions and the missing screws are not making it easy.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Tom, are you sure these instructions are in the right order? I think we put this big panel in the wrong way.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Let me see. Yes, the holes are at the top. They should be at the bottom.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Great. So we have to take it apart again.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Don't worry. We only used four screws. Pass me the small screwdriver, please.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Here. Be careful — one screw is already a bit damaged.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: I noticed. The box said there should be twenty-four screws, but I've only counted twenty-two.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Are you serious? Maybe they fell on the floor when we opened the box.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Let's check under the sofa. They probably rolled there. Otherwise I have to walk to the hardware shop.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Wait — look, there are two screws near my foot. That's all of them.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Perfect. Now let's try again, slowly this time. Hold the side panel straight while I screw in the top.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: How long do you think this will take? I'm getting hungry.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Maybe another hour. After we finish, I'll order pizza. You choose the place.

body.speaker: Sara
body.text: Deal. By the way, where are we going to put it? Against the long wall or near the window?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: I'd say the long wall. The window side gets too much sun, and the sun is bad for our books.

annotations.exampleEn: Read the instructions before you start.
The back panel of the cupboard is broken.
We had to take it apart and try again.
These screws are too short for the wall.
Can I borrow your small screwdriver?
The box was a little damaged in delivery.
The coin rolled under the chair.
There's a hardware shop on the next street.
Please hold the side panel straight while I drill.

annotations.meaningVi: tôi nghĩ là, tôi cho rằng
annotations.exampleEn: I'd say we need a bigger sofa.

annotations.meaningVi: không tốt cho sách của chúng ta
annotations.exampleEn: Direct sunlight is bad for our books.

grammarNotes.title: 'should' vs 'have to' for instructions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'should + V' đưa ra lời khuyên hay nói điều gì là đúng/nên thế nào. 'have to + V' diễn tả điều bắt buộc. Trong bài: 'They should be at the bottom' (đúng ra phải ở dưới); 'we have to take it apart again' (bắt buộc phải tháo ra).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'should + verb' for what is correct or advisable, and 'have to + verb' for what is necessary or required. From the dialogue: 'They should be at the bottom' (this is the correct position) vs 'We have to take it apart again' (this is necessary).

grammarNotes.title: Future with 'will' for promises and offers
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will' (hoặc dạng rút gọn 'I'll') để đưa ra lời hứa hoặc đề nghị ngay lúc nói. Trong bài: 'I'll order pizza' và 'I'll screw in the top'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will' (often 'I'll') for promises and offers made at the moment of speaking. From the dialogue: 'I'll order pizza' is a spontaneous promise.

translationVi:
Sara: Tom, anh có chắc hướng dẫn đúng thứ tự không? Em nghĩ mình đã lắp tấm lớn này ngược rồi.
Tom: Để anh xem. Đúng vậy, các lỗ đang ở phía trên. Lẽ ra phải ở dưới.
Sara: Hay nhỉ. Vậy mình phải tháo ra lại.
Tom: Đừng lo. Mới chỉ vặn bốn con ốc. Đưa anh cái tua vít nhỏ.
Sara: Đây. Cẩn thận — một con ốc đã hơi hỏng rồi.
Tom: Anh thấy rồi. Trên hộp ghi phải có hai mươi tư con ốc, nhưng anh chỉ đếm được hai mươi hai.
Sara: Anh nói thật à? Có thể chúng rơi xuống sàn lúc mình mở hộp.
Tom: Cùng tìm dưới ghế sofa xem. Chắc chúng lăn vào đó. Không thì anh phải đi bộ đến cửa hàng đồ kim khí.
Sara: Khoan — nhìn này, có hai con ngay gần chân em. Đủ rồi.
Tom: Tuyệt. Giờ mình thử lại, lần này chậm thôi. Em giữ tấm bên đứng thẳng để anh vặn ốc trên.
Sara: Anh nghĩ việc này còn bao lâu? Em đói rồi.
Tom: Chắc thêm một giờ nữa. Xong xuôi, anh sẽ đặt pizza. Em chọn quán.
Sara: Chốt. À mà, mình đặt nó ở đâu? Sát tường dài hay gần cửa sổ?
Tom: Anh nghĩ là sát tường dài. Phía cửa sổ nắng nhiều quá, mà nắng không tốt cho sách.

questions.prompt: What is the main problem at the start of the dialogue?
options: They put a big panel in the wrong way
Sara forgot to buy the bookshelf
questions.explanation: 'I think we put this big panel in the wrong way.'
questions.hint: Listen for what Sara says is wrong with the panel.
Listen for Tom's correction about the holes.

questions.prompt: How many screws had they used before noticing the mistake?
questions.hint: Listen for a small number used so far.

questions.prompt: How many screws should there be in total, and how many did Tom count?
options: Twenty should be there; he counted eighteen
Twenty-four should be there; he counted twenty-two
Thirty should be there; he counted twenty-eight
Twelve should be there; he counted ten
questions.explanation: 'there should be twenty-four screws, but I've only counted twenty-two.'
questions.hint: Listen for two numbers in a single line.

questions.prompt: Where do they find the two missing screws?
questions.explanation: 'look, there are two screws near my foot.'
questions.hint: Listen for where Sara spots them.

questions.prompt: Where would Tom have to go if they couldn't find the screws?
questions.explanation: 'Otherwise I have to walk to the hardware shop.'
questions.hint: Listen for a shop that sells tools and screws.

questions.prompt: What does Tom ask Sara to do during the next try?
questions.explanation: 'Hold the side panel straight while I screw in the top.'
questions.hint: Listen for an instruction about a panel.

questions.prompt: What does Tom promise to do after they finish?
questions.hint: Listen for a promise about food.

questions.prompt: Where do they decide to put the bookshelf, and why?
options: Against the long wall, because the window side gets too much sun
Near the window, because it looks nicer
In the kitchen, because there is more space
In the corner, because it is hidden
questions.explanation: 'I'd say the long wall. The window side gets too much sun, and the sun is bad for our books.'
questions.hint: Listen for two pieces of information: the place AND the reason.

questions.prompt: What is Tom and Sara's general mood during the dialogue?
options: Cooperative even when things go wrong
questions.explanation: They make mistakes, run out of screws, and get hungry, but they help each other and joke about pizza.
questions.hint: Think about how they treat each other overall, not just one line.

cloze.template:

Tom: Let me see. Yes, the holes are at the top. They {{b2}} be at the bottom.
Sara: Great. So we have to take it {{b3}} again.
Tom: Don't worry. We only used four screws. Pass me the small {{b4}}, please.
Sara: Here. Be careful — one screw is already a bit damaged.
Tom: I noticed. The box said there should be twenty-four screws, but I've only {{b5}} twenty-two.
Sara: Are you serious? Maybe they fell on the floor when we opened the box.
Tom: Let's check under the sofa. They probably {{b6}} there. Otherwise I have to walk to the hardware shop.
Sara: Wait — look, there are two screws near my foot. That's all of them.
Tom: Perfect. Now let's try again, slowly this time. Hold the side panel {{b7}} while I screw in the top.
Sara: How long do you think this will take? I'm getting {{b8}}.
Tom: Maybe another hour. After we finish, I'll {{b9}} pizza. You choose the place.
Sara: Deal. By the way, where are we going to put it? Against the long wall or near the window?
Tom: I'd say the long wall. The window side gets too much {{b10}}, and the sun is bad for our books.

blanks.explanation: 'instructions' — written guidance for how to build something.
'should' — the correct position for the holes.
'apart' — 'take it apart' means to separate the pieces.
'screwdriver' — the tool used to turn screws.
'rolled' — small round screws roll on a flat floor.
'order' — to order food from a restaurant.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Sara and Tom solve their problem by helping each other instead of getting angry. When something goes wrong at home — broken furniture, a missing item, a small mistake — what makes the difference between a fight and a calm fix? Is it the people, the moment, or both?
